D1_no_renormalisable_channel.conclusion: M_R only via d=5 (16 16bar_H)^2/Lambda (16_H is E8-allowed; v247)

D4_23smooth_theorem.closure: products/quotients of {2,3}-smooth rationals stay {2,3}-smooth -> 5/3 unreachable from any channel combination
D4_23smooth_theorem.unique_natural_5: k_Y = Tr16 Y^2 / Tr16 T3L^2 = 5/3 (full-multiplet trace, v470)
D4_23smooth_theorem.k_Y_decoupled_from_nu_c: Y(nu^c) = 0 exactly -> the only 5-carrying direction cannot touch M_R

D5_generation_blindness.statement: M_R = G * c_ij with G a family-space scalar; any generation ratio is c-texture, never Clebsch

D6_compiler_flavor_inventory.needed_slot: gen3 vs unstructured base (v482: M_R3 = (3/5) c3^3 Mbar with gen1 = gen2 = base)
D6_compiler_flavor_inventory.typing: wrong-slot hits recorded as inventory; no mechanism links M_R to address tables; declined per v354/v355

VERDICT: THE 3/5 CLEBSCH DOOR CLOSES: no renormalisable channel (126 E8-forbidden); all nu^c d=5 channel weights are {2,3}-smooth, so no channel combination reaches 5/3; the unique 5-carrying group number k_Y = 5/3 is decoupled (Y(nu^c) = 0); Clebsches are generation-blind by construction; compiler-flavor 3/5's sit in wrong slots.  The r ~ 5/3 rescue stays a numerical coincidence without mechanism -- the honest endpoint remains the v481 window candidate.
